syllabus.topic: Course Context, Reminders, and Lecture Roadmap
sub_points: Positioning of the lecture within Pattern Recognition (EE5907) and expected background
Quick reminders of prior material needed for the week (e.g., basic linear algebra/probability notions used later)
Overview of the session flow: supervised feature learning leading to LDA and its extensions

syllabus.topic: Pattern Representation Learning and Supervised Feature Learning Setup
sub_points: Motivation for learning feature representations for recognition tasks
Difference between unsupervised vs. supervised feature learning objectives
How to define “good” supervised criteria: using class structure to guide projections/embeddings

syllabus.topic: Class Separability: Between-Class and Within-Class Scatter Measures
sub_points: Definition and intuition of between-class distance/separation and its role in classification
Within-class compactness vs. between-class dispersion as competing objectives
Scatter matrices (within-class scatter Sw, between-class scatter Sb, total scatter St) and their computation
Historical notes motivating Fisher’s approach to discriminative projection

syllabus.topic: Fisher’s Linear Discriminant Analysis (Two-Class): Criterion and Optimization
sub_points: Problem statement: find a projection direction that best separates class populations
Fisher criterion J(w): maximize between-class separation while minimizing within-class variance
Derivation of the optimal projection direction and rewriting the criterion in matrix form
Connection to the generalized eigenvalue problem: Sb w = λ Sw w and conditions for solvability

syllabus.topic: LDA in Practice: Examples and Multi-Class/Multiple-Projection Extension
sub_points: Worked examples illustrating LDA projection and resulting separability
Extension from a single projection to multiple discriminant directions (subspace LDA)
Use of determinant/trace-based objectives for multi-dimensional projections
Practical computation pipeline for LDA projection vectors from training data
Rank limitation and why the number of LDA directions is at most min(d, C−1)

syllabus.topic: PCA vs. LDA for Classification: Empirical Comparisons and Data-Regime Effects
sub_points: Face recognition example setup (e.g., YALE database) as a motivating application
Conceptual contrast: PCA maximizes variance; LDA maximizes class separability
When LDA may or may not outperform PCA (e.g., small-sample issues, overfitting risk)
Case study comparisons under small vs. large training dataset regimes
Reinforcement of dimensionality limits for LDA features: at most min(d, C−1)

syllabus.topic: General Framework: Graph Embedding (GE) for Feature Learning
sub_points: Unifying view: casting feature learning methods as graph/embedding optimization problems
GE problem formulation (objective + constraints) and interpretation in terms of preserving desired relations
Equivalence/proof ideas for reformulating constraints to standard optimization forms
Linearization procedures to obtain tractable linear solutions (often leading to eigenproblems)
Kernelization to enable nonlinear embeddings in high-dimensional feature spaces

syllabus.topic: GE-Based Algorithms: Marginal Fisher Analysis (MFA) and Face Recognition Experiment
sub_points: Designing a new discriminative algorithm under the GE framework
Marginal Fisher Analysis (MFA): local neighborhood graphs for within-class compactness and between-class margin maximization
How MFA relates to/extends classical LDA by focusing on marginal (local) structures rather than global class statistics
Experimental pipeline and evaluation for face recognition using learned embeddings
Pointers to foundational references (Graph Embedding and Extensions by Yan, Xu et al.)
